title: Sword Art Online: Fatal Bullet
notes: The game works flawlessly, you will have to mess around with the settings to set the FPS cap back to 60, however, once that is done it runs as well as it did on Windows.
Game launches and runs perfectly fine, with no extra configuration required.
title: Sword Art Online: Fatal Bullet

notes: Using DXVK 0.80 got rid of the mouse input lag that at times occured, previously. Setting AA to off and Shadows to low yields constant 60FPS. High level shadows seem to have a very significant impact on performance.
title: Sword Art Online: Fatal Bullet

notes: Can't start the game ! Error: "The server is not reachable. Check your internet connection and click Retry"
title: Sword Art Online: Fatal Bullet

notes: If any one has Server is not reachable errors use the Ubuntu based runtimes, on first run. DO NOT use Native runtime on DRM protected games for the first time. That Aside. The game runs flawlessly on NVIDIA, solid frames which is what I like to see. Load times are a bit slow but that might be from btrfs. TLDL Load first with normal runtime, play game, and have fun.
title: Sword Art Online: Fatal Bullet

notes: Runs flawlessly when frame rate is set to 6o fps.
title: Sword Art Online: Fatal Bullet

notes:
Installed using Steam runtime (not Steam native). I only had to set "PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 %command%" in the launch options to get it running. Without this, I get the famous "DX11 feature level 10.0 is required to run the engine" error.

I played until tutorial's end, everything runs smoothly. Note that I had to disable my other monitors, because the mouse cursors leaves the game's window.
title: Sword Art Online: Fatal Bullet

notes: Infrequent crashes, dialog says related to shaders, so possibly GPU or drivers. Infrequent slow downs and stuttering.
title: Sword Art Online: Fatal Bullet

notes: The game runs fine without any tweaking, however in my case there are frame drops on The Old South, near the City Ruins. I have reduced graphics and deactivated depth of field, but frame drops were still present on that area.  Though, the other areas are totally okay.
title: Sword Art Online: Fatal Bullet

notes: Runs fine with no tweaks necessary, but online play seems to be inaccessible.
title: Sword Art Online: Fatal Bullet

notes:
It’s possible to launch the game, however it’s unplayable with Proton 3.16-6. 
When it launches, it gets stuck immediately on a black screen that cannot be bypassed with button presses. This worked flawlessly on version 3.16-4, so I recommend downgrading to that version, through steam settings.
